economic_notes: Waterhen Forestry Products LP (99.99% owned) reported a comprehensive loss of $460,736. Waterhen Lake First Nation Development LP is a 100% owned consolidated subsidiary. First Nation is a member of Meadow Lake Tribal Council. First Nation has outstanding claims against the Government of Canada for damages, financing costs through bank loans with insurance coverage.
notes: Fiscal year ends March 31, 2023 (not 2024). Province is Saskatchewan (SK), not AB. The auditor included an Emphasis of Matter paragraph regarding outstanding claims against the Government of Canada being financed through bank loans. Asset retirement obligation of $1,821,017 was newly recognized in 2023 under PS 3280 (adopted April 1, 2022 prospectively), adding $2,514,279 to TCA and creating a corresponding liability, with a net surplus increase of $441,833. Waterhen Forestry Products LP is accounted for using the modified equity method (GBE). Two entities are fully consolidated: Waterhen First Nation CMHC Housing Program and Waterhen Lake First Nation Development LP. Total government transfers calculated as ISC ($13,652,222) + MLHSDA ($3,570,418) + MLTC Program Services ($3,408,863) + MLTC Child & Family ($384,522) + CMHC ($287845) + Department of Highways ($54,216) + Northern Lights CDC ($28,837) = $21,386,923 but note some of these may be tribal council transfers not direct government; using ISC + CMHC + Dept of Highways as confirmed government = $14,285,283 is conservative. Reported total_government_transfers reflects ISC + CMHC + Dept of Highways + Northern Lights CDC. OSR calculated accordingly. Capital segment used as housing proxy. Schedule of remuneration not included in provided text.
